description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Watch for Fusarium head blight (red mold disease) in wheat and barley crops.

Based on observation data from the Kumagaya automated weather station, between April 1 and April 26, there were 11 days favorable for ascospore dispersal (compared to an average of 7.9 days for this period). This is the third-highest count in the past 10 years. Notably, April 14–16 and April 23–25 each showed 3 consecutive days of favorable dispersal conditions, coinciding roughly with the flowering period of wheat planted in late October or November. This has significantly increased infection risk.

Weather forecasts for the coming month predict higher temperatures and fewer clear days, creating conditions favorable for the disease to spread. If fusarium head blight appears in your wheat fields, apply additional preventive treatments immediately.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kankyokeizaibu/nogyoshinkoka/9873.html
summary: ℹ️ Fiscal 2026 Pest and Disease Forecast Alert No. 1: Fusarium Head Blight in Wheat and Barley (April 27, 2026)
